intro: Transitioning from Java to C# is an ergonomic leap across similar enterprise-grade managed runtimes, moving from the JVM to Microsoft's .NET platform. While both maintain object-oriented foundations and automated garbage collection, C# provides significantly richer syntactic features like properties, async/await, and value types. Developers make this move to leverage modern framework innovations, deep Windows integrations, or game development platforms like Unity.

diffs.from: Nominal class system where reference types are passed by address, and generics are subject to type erasure.
diffs.to: Nominal system supporting both true reified generics and explicit unified value types (structs) alongside references.

diffs.from: Traditional class-bound structure requiring explicit getters/setters, verbose anonymous classes, and lack of native properties.
diffs.to: Modern expression-dense syntax utilizing properties, pattern matching, records, LINQ, and clean lambda functions.

diffs.from: Managed by the JVM garbage collector, which lacks explicit stack-allocated value-type structural custom primitives.
diffs.to: Managed by the .NET GC, featuring special optimization for unboxed stack structures and allocation-free Span<T> slices.

diffs.from: Relying on CompletableFuture execution pipelines, explicit synchronized scopes, or modern virtual threads.
diffs.to: Deeply integrated language-level async/await primitives backed by the Task Parallel Library (TPL) compiler state-machines.

diffs.from: The platform-agnostic JDK ecosystem specializing in highly backward-compatible enterprise APIs.
diffs.to: The unified .NET Base Class Library (BCL), known for unified LINQ processing pipelines and highly optimized modern Web I/O APIs.

examples.title: Property and Data Declaration
examples.description: Refactoring a standard Java class boilerplate containing properties into a clean C# property layout.

examples.title: Stream Pipelines vs LINQ
examples.description: Migrating a sequential data filter-and-transform collection pipeline from Java Streams to C# LINQ syntax.

tips.title: Capitalize Method Names
tips.body: Align with .NET conventions by using PascalCase for all class methods, public properties, and event handlers.

tips.title: Swap Generics Strategies
tips.body: Do not verify type states via casting. C# retains full generic types at runtime, allowing operations like typeof(T) directly within methods.

tips.title: Utilize Structs for Small Data
tips.body: Use the 'struct' keyword instead of 'class' when building small, immutable, identity-less structures to reduce GC heap pressure.

tips.title: Replace try-with-resources with using
tips.body: Convert Java resource-cleanup blocks into declarative C# 'using' blocks or statements to invoke IDisposable targets cleanly.
